chunks.text:
About Son Bui
Who is Son Bui
Son Bui (full name Bui Thanh Son) is a Software Engineer in Test (SDET) based in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam, and open to remote work worldwide. His focus is intelligent test platforms, framework architecture, and testing infrastructure that make software quality scalable across an engineering organization.

His one-line positioning: **"I build AI-powered automation platforms — not just automated tests."**

meta.title: About Son Bui
meta.summary: Software Engineer in Test focused on AI-powered automation platforms, framework architecture, and testing infrastructure that scale software quality across an engineering organization.

chunks.text:
About Son Bui
What makes his work distinctive
Son treats automation as software engineering rather than script writing. He measures success not by the number of tests written, but by how much repetitive engineering effort his platforms eliminate for the whole team. His signature project is an AI-powered, multi-agent test-automation platform that takes a Jira ticket all the way to a production-ready pull request.

meta.title: About Son Bui
meta.summary: Software Engineer in Test focused on AI-powered automation platforms, framework architecture, and testing infrastructure that scale software quality across an engineering organization.

chunks.text:
About Son Bui
Areas of strength
He works across the testing lifecycle and the stack: reusable Playwright + TypeScript frameworks, cross-platform desktop testing with Squish, CI/CD and automation infrastructure, and AI agent systems built on the Claude Agent SDK and the Model Context Protocol (MCP). He also has full-stack engineering experience (Java, Spring Boot, MySQL, Angular/React) that informs how he designs testable systems.

Availability
Son is open to Senior SDET and Test Platform engineering roles, remote-friendly. He can be reached at sonbuithanh306@gmail.com, on GitHub at github.com/sontbui, and on LinkedIn.

chunks.text:
Engineering Principles & Philosophy
Core philosophy
"Automation is software engineering. I measure success not by the number of tests I write, but by how much engineering effort my platforms eliminate." Every automation solution should reduce engineering effort in the long term — not simply make today's test pass.

meta.title: Engineering Principles & Philosophy
meta.summary: The principles that guide how Son Bui approaches software quality and automation — treating automation as software engineering that reduces long-term effort.

chunks.text:
Engineering Principles & Philosophy
Principles Son follows
- **Build maintainable systems over quick fixes.** Design for the teammate who extends it in six months, not the demo today.
- **Design reusable frameworks, not duplicated solutions.** Solve a class of problems once and let the platform scale it.
- **Automate workflows, not just test cases.** The biggest wins come from removing whole steps, not scripting one.
- **Developer experience is a first-class concern.** Fast feedback and clear reporting are what make automation actually get used.
- **Use AI to augment engineering, not replace thinking.** AI handles the repetitive; engineers keep ownership of judgment.

meta.title: Engineering Principles & Philosophy
meta.summary: The principles that guide how Son Bui approaches software quality and automation — treating automation as software engineering that reduces long-term effort.

chunks.text:
Engineering Principles & Philosophy
How Son approaches software quality
He treats a test framework as a product for engineers: clear fixtures, fast feedback, and readable reports drive real adoption. He favours fixture-driven architecture with shared utilities over per-suite convenience — more upfront design, but reuse and maintainability that compound across every product. Reliability is engineered explicitly through flaky-test detection, retry, quarantine, and trend analysis.

meta.title: Engineering Principles & Philosophy
meta.summary: The principles that guide how Son Bui approaches software quality and automation — treating automation as software engineering that reduces long-term effort.

chunks.text:
Engineering Principles & Philosophy
Why Software Engineer in Test
Son is drawn to the leverage of the role: instead of testing one feature, he builds the platforms and frameworks that let an entire organization ship quality faster. He enjoys problems where the win is removing repetitive engineering — turning a manual, linear-effort workflow into a scalable system.

Work Experience
Associate Software Engineer in Test (SDET) — OPSWAT Vietnam
**Industry:** Enterprise Cybersecurity · **Period:** Nov 2024 — Present

At OPSWAT, Son designed and built the team's reusable Playwright + TypeScript framework, moving the team from a fully manual process to scalable hybrid automation. He architected an AI-powered automation proof-of-concept (Claude Agent SDK, MCP, n8n) that automates the path from a Jira ticket to a pull request.

He established automation architecture and engineering standards and reviewed automation design across the team. His work grew automation coverage from 0 to roughly 80% with around 700 cross-platform tests, cut regression execution time by about 70%, and reduced flaky tests from roughly 150 to fewer than 20.

**Technologies:** Playwright, TypeScript, Squish, Claude Agent SDK, MCP, n8n, CI/CD.

chunks.text:
AI-Powered Test Automation Platform
Overview
The AI-Powered Test Automation Platform is Son's flagship project (a proof of concept, actively expanding). It is a multi-agent system that automates the journey from a Jira ticket to a production-ready automation pull request — requirement analysis, test generation, execution, failure triage, and delivery, orchestrated end to end.

meta.title: AI-Powered Test Automation Platform
meta.summary: A multi-agent system that automates the journey from a Jira ticket to a production-ready automation pull request — Son Bui's flagship project.

chunks.text:
AI-Powered Test Automation Platform
The problem
Test automation is usually treated as a manual engineering workflow: read the ticket, understand requirements, write the test, run it, debug the failure, open the PR. Every step is repetitive, and every engineer does it slightly differently — so effort scales linearly with the work.

meta.title: AI-Powered Test Automation Platform
meta.summary: A multi-agent system that automates the journey from a Jira ticket to a production-ready automation pull request — Son Bui's flagship project.

chunks.text:
AI-Powered Test Automation Platform
The insight
Treat AI not as a coding assistant but as an engineering component: specialized agents that each own a stage of the lifecycle and collaborate through a shared orchestration layer, with context supplied by the Model Context Protocol (MCP). The goal isn't more tests; it's removing the repetitive engineering between a requirement and its verification.

meta.title: AI-Powered Test Automation Platform
meta.summary: A multi-agent system that automates the journey from a Jira ticket to a production-ready automation pull request — Son Bui's flagship project.

chunks.text:
AI-Powered Test Automation Platform
Agent orchestration architecture
Context enters through **Jira MCP** and **Confluence MCP** connectors into an **Orchestrator** built on the Claude Agent SDK, n8n, and YAML workflows. The orchestrator coordinates six specialized agents and emits artifacts: Playwright tests, a Git branch and pull request, and a Jira comment. A self-correction loop feeds fixes from the Failure Analyst back to the Test Generator.

The six agents:

- **Requirement Analyst** — normalizes the ticket into testable requirements.
- **Knowledge Retriever** — pulls project context from Jira & Confluence.
- **Test Generator** — writes Playwright (TypeScript) automation.
- **Executor** — runs the suite and captures results.
- **Failure Analyst** — separates automation issues from real defects.
- **PR Author** — creates the branch, commits, PR, and Jira comment.

meta.title: AI-Powered Test Automation Platform
meta.summary: A multi-agent system that automates the journey from a Jira ticket to a production-ready automation pull request — Son Bui's flagship project.

chunks.text:
AI-Powered Test Automation Platform
Execution sequence (Ticket → PR)
1. **Ticket received** (Jira MCP) — requirements normalized into a testable spec.
2. **Context retrieved** — relevant project knowledge pulled from Jira & Confluence to ground generation.
3. **Tests generated** — Playwright TypeScript tests authored from requirements and context.
4. **Suite executed** — automation runs; results, traces, and failures collected.
5. **Failures triaged** — each failure classified as automation issue vs. genuine product defect.
6. **Self-correction loop** — flaky or fixable automation is corrected and re-run automatically.
7. **Delivered** — branch, commits, and PR created and the Jira ticket updated, ready for human review.

meta.title: AI-Powered Test Automation Platform
meta.summary: A multi-agent system that automates the journey from a Jira ticket to a production-ready automation pull request — Son Bui's flagship project.

chunks.text:
AI-Powered Test Automation Platform
Engineering decisions & trade-offs
- **AI as an engineering component, not an assistant.** Each agent owns one lifecycle stage with a clear contract, so the system is composable and debuggable — not a single opaque prompt.
- **MCP for context, not scraping.** MCP gives agents structured access to Jira & Confluence, keeping context accurate and integration maintainable.
- **Human-in-the-loop at the PR.** The platform stops at a pull request; delivery stays reviewable. AI does the repetitive work, engineers own the merge.
- **Orchestration via n8n + YAML.** Declarative workflows make the pipeline transparent and easy to extend without rewriting agent logic.

Results (POC)
- ~20 minutes from ticket to PR for a typical task (~10 test cases).
- 6 specialized agents across the lifecycle.
- End-to-end with no manual step in between.

What's next
Broaden coverage beyond Playwright to desktop/API generation; add confidence scoring so the platform knows when to defer to a human; and richer failure analytics feeding suite-health trends.

Stack
Claude Agent SDK, Model Context Protocol, Jira MCP, Confluence MCP, Playwright, TypeScript, n8n, YAML, Git automation.

meta.title: AI-Powered Test Automation Platform
meta.summary: A multi-agent system that automates the journey from a Jira ticket to a production-ready automation pull request — Son Bui's flagship project.

chunks.text:
Enterprise Playwright Automation Framework
Overview
A reusable Playwright + TypeScript framework covering UI, API, and backend testing that moved the team at OPSWAT from a fully manual process to scalable hybrid automation. This is an enterprise project; it is presented through original architecture, without proprietary detail.

The problem
Manual regression didn't scale and produced inconsistent coverage across enterprise products spanning multiple platforms.

Key trade-off
Son chose a fixture-driven architecture with shared utilities over per-suite convenience — more upfront design, but reuse and maintainability that compound across every product.

Lesson
A framework is a product for engineers. Developer experience — clear fixtures, fast feedback, readable reports — is what drives real adoption.

meta.title: Enterprise Playwright Automation Framework
meta.summary: A reusable Playwright + TypeScript framework covering UI, API, and backend testing that moved an enterprise team from manual testing to scalable hybrid automation.

chunks.text:
Enterprise Playwright Automation Framework
Layered architecture
- **Test Specs** (UI · API · Backend) — readable, intent-focused test cases.
- **Fixtures** (lifecycle) — setup/teardown, auth, shared state.
- **Page & Component Objects** (abstraction) — encapsulated selectors and interactions.
- **Core Utilities** (shared) — helpers, data builders, API clients.
- **Config & Reporting** (platform) — environments, parallelism, CI integration.

meta.title: Enterprise Playwright Automation Framework
meta.summary: A reusable Playwright + TypeScript framework covering UI, API, and backend testing that moved an enterprise team from manual testing to scalable hybrid automation.

chunks.text:
Enterprise Playwright Automation Framework
Parallel execution
One codebase fans out into N shards and merges into a single report, keeping feedback fast as the suite grows. This is the project that demonstrates Son's architecture thinking most directly: designing for reuse, composition, and developer experience rather than one-off scripts.

meta.title: Enterprise Playwright Automation Framework
meta.summary: A reusable Playwright + TypeScript framework covering UI, API, and backend testing that moved an enterprise team from manual testing to scalable hybrid automation.

chunks.text:
Full-Stack E-Commerce Platform
Overview
A full-stack personal project built to deepen backend engineering — REST API design, authentication, and automated UI testing — applying software-engineering principles beyond testing. Source is on GitHub at github.com/sontbui/E-commerce-Platform.

meta.title: Full-Stack E-Commerce Platform
meta.summary: A personal full-stack project built to deepen backend engineering — REST API design, authentication, and automated UI testing — applying software-engineering principles beyond testing.

chunks.text:
Full-Stack E-Commerce Platform
Does Son have backend experience?
Yes. This project is the clearest evidence: Son designed and built a **Spring Boot REST API** with **Spring Security and JWT** authentication over a **MySQL** relational store, with an **Angular** single-page front end (TypeScript · Material UI). It demonstrates real backend engineering — API design, auth, and data modelling — not just testing.

3-tier architecture
- **Angular SPA** (TypeScript · Material UI) — the client tier.
- **Spring Boot REST API** (Spring Security · JWT) — the application tier.
- **MySQL** — the relational data store.

Testing
Selenium exercises the UI end-to-end, giving automated regression across the full stack — Son applying his testing discipline to his own product.

meta.title: Full-Stack E-Commerce Platform
meta.summary: A personal full-stack project built to deepen backend engineering — REST API design, authentication, and automated UI testing — applying software-engineering principles beyond testing.

chunks.text:
Enterprise Test Infrastructure
Overview
The platform behind the tests: Playwright (web) and Squish (desktop) unified into one cross-platform execution and reporting ecosystem across Windows, macOS, and Linux. This is a representative model of enterprise work, without proprietary detail.

Context
Cybersecurity products aren't standard web apps — many are desktop applications that must be validated across three operating systems, which web-only tooling can't cover.

meta.title: Enterprise Test Infrastructure
meta.summary: The cross-platform platform behind the tests — Playwright (web) and Squish (desktop) unified into one execution and reporting ecosystem across Windows, macOS, and Linux.

chunks.text:
Enterprise Test Infrastructure
Approach
Playwright for web and Squish for desktop/Qt GUIs feed a shared orchestration and test-data layer, so a heterogeneous suite reads as one signal to the team. Execution fans out across Windows, macOS, and Linux, then converges into unified reporting and flaky detection.

Reliability
A flaky-test feedback loop — retry, quarantine, and trend analysis — took the suite from roughly 150 unstable tests to fewer than 20.

System architecture
Runners (Playwright for web/API, Squish for desktop/Qt) → a shared Orchestration & Test-Data Layer → execution across the Windows / macOS / Linux matrix → Unified Reporting and Flaky Detection.

meta.title: Enterprise Test Infrastructure
meta.summary: The cross-platform platform behind the tests — Playwright (web) and Squish (desktop) unified into one execution and reporting ecosystem across Windows, macOS, and Linux.

chunks.text:
Enterprise Test Infrastructure
CI/CD pipeline
Commit → Build → OS Matrix → Parallel Run → Aggregate → Flaky Gate. This is Son's cross-platform testing and CI/CD infrastructure experience: making a diverse, multi-OS suite behave like a single reliable signal.

meta.title: Enterprise Test Infrastructure
meta.summary: The cross-platform platform behind the tests — Playwright (web) and Squish (desktop) unified into one execution and reporting ecosystem across Windows, macOS, and Linux.
